title: 30 to hit North American theaters in January 2014
display_title: 30 to hit North American theaters in January 2014
wikitext:
[]: 3.0 to hit North American theaters in January 2014             Quote Post by hui43210 » Mon Nov 11, 2013 1:25 pm http://ow.ly/i/3GVbp Hooray!

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Mon Nov 11, 2013 1:31 pm I was JUST about it post this Aww YEAH! I can't wait edit: Funimation will "announce dates & cities in a few weeks" here's hoping it's sooner rather than later.

[]:             Quote Post by Black Swordsman » Mon Nov 11, 2013 2:03 pm Wish this was more than the USA...would love if this came to Scotland :(

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Nov 11, 2013 2:15 pm I wish they weren't doing it at all. I really just want them to cut to the chase and release a domestic copy.

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Mon Nov 11, 2013 2:18 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: I wish they weren't doing it at all. I really just they to cut to the chase and release a domestic copy.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Mon Nov 11, 2013 2:21 pm Some "when"s and "where"s would be nice I may be going to Katsucon in February so it'd be great to know if it's being screened there (would save me time and money going to see it elsewhere) I know you're not much for the cinema experience Eva Monkey but uh these big silly movies were kind of made to be seen on the big screen

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Mon Nov 11, 2013 4:06 pm I'd love to see this on the big screen if I didn't have to suffer through the insufferable dubs. Oh well. I wouldn't worry too much about a theatrical release pushing the home video release back or anything. These small theatrical releases are almost purely for show & I'm positive we'll get the Bluray on that earlier mentioned February date or sometime in March. Still impressive how fast all this 3.0 stuff has moved. At around this point during the 2.0 release campaign 2.22 had JUST hit Japanese Domestic Bluray/DVD.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Mon Nov 11, 2013 4:17 pm Kind of shocked that people still bitch about the dubs for Rebuild . Like, I can't really imagine a mindset whereby they are not at least watchable , let alone an active deterrent from the otherwise enjoyable experience of seeing the movies on the big screen. Oh well, weebs gonna weeb I guess. If it makes you feel any better though GP the theatrical runs for both previous films have shown both dubbed and subtitled versions depending on location.

[]:             Quote Post by SSJ Spartan01 » Mon Nov 11, 2013 4:26 pm Excited? Excited. . . doesn't. . .even. . .freakin. . . DESCRIBE IT! I'll definitely be checking this out. The cinema experience will just make it 10x better. I'm cool on waiting for the DVD release because I'll save it for the inevitable tetralogy blu-rei pack.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Mon Nov 11, 2013 4:32 pm Outside of Spike Spencer & Allison Keith the cast is pretty terrible across the boards as far as being actors go. And yes, I'm aware they do both subbed & dubbed. With any luck my area will get a sub option. Also - let's not be pedantic here. This outlook of mine on dubbing is not strictly limited to fucking anime. If the filmmakers made that specific movie or TV show in another language no matter what language that may be - English, French, Spanish, Japanese, German, Dutch, Hindu, freaking Martian - than that's how I want to watch it. I feel that way about live action feature films, television & anime. It's not being a "weeb".

[]:             Quote Post by WunderBah » Mon Nov 11, 2013 5:04 pm I could imagine the huge slander this film would get if it aired along with Toonami's December movie nights next month.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Mon Nov 11, 2013 5:05 pm Most non-weebs do not have such a powerful aversion to watching Japanese cartoons in English, even when they are dubbed with reasonable competency, that it prevents them from deriving pleasure from such an experience altogether. Anyway, my problem with watching the movie on Toonami, as it is with watching any other movie on basic cable, is that I cannot tolerate the thought of watching a film with commercial breaks shoehorned in.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Nov 11, 2013 5:57 pm gatotsu911 wrote: Oh well, weebs gonna weeb I guess.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Mon Nov 11, 2013 5:57 pm lol, "edgy"

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Nov 11, 2013 6:03 pm What does one call someone else who watches a French film in it's original French language I wonder... "Wrenches?" Does this make me a Wrench as well as a Weeb? What if I liked watching Downfall in it's original German language? I guess that would simply make me a Nazi or something. Heheh. Yeah, I guess we are the scum of the Earth.

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Mon Nov 11, 2013 6:21 pm I watch some anime with my mom when I feel like spending free time with her. It's our way of bonding, I told her long ago after we watched 1.0 & 2.0 together that we're going to go see 3.0 theaters. It's gonna be so AWESOME! Me and my mom, gonna go watch some Eva!

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Nov 11, 2013 8:08 pm Seeing this in theaters for the second time is going to be awesome. It's going to be hard to wait for this.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Mon Nov 11, 2013 8:16 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: What does one call someone else who watches a French film in it's original French language I wonder... "Wrenches?" Does this make me a Wrench as well as a Weeb? What if I liked watching Downfall in it's original German language? I guess that would simply make me a Nazi or something. Heheh. Yeah, I guess we are the scum of the Earth.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Mon Nov 11, 2013 8:19 pm Isn't there a thread for "Sub Vs. Dub"? Can't any future posts made about this pointless argument be moved there instead?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Nov 11, 2013 8:29 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: Isn't there a thread for "Sub Vs. Dub"? Can't any future posts made about this pointless argument be moved there instead?
